title: Getting started with toolero
text: toolero small, opinionated toolkit designed make first steps R project faster consistent. targets researchers analysts want spend less time setup time work . package currently provides two functions: init_project() — creates new R project standard folder structure, optionally initializes renv git read_clean_csv() — reads CSV file cleans column names one step functions designed around simple idea: decisions make start project — organized, data read , dependencies tracked — outsized effect maintainable reproducible project turns . toolero tries make right defaults easy reach .

title: Getting started with toolero
text: can install toolero CRAN: install development version GitHub:
code: install.packages("toolero") pak::pak("erwinlares/toolero")

title: Getting started with toolero
text: Starting new R project usually means manual steps every time: create folder, set RStudio project, create subdirectories data scripts, initialize renv, initialize git. None steps hard , skipping — especially early — tends create friction later. project without renv harder share. project without git harder recover. project without consistent folder structure harder hand .

title: Getting started with toolero
text: init_project() handles single call: creates new RStudio project specified path following folder structure already place: structure? folder layout opinionated arbitrary. Separating data/ data-raw/ makes clear files original processed. Keeping R/ distinct scripts/ encourages moving reusable logic functions time, natural step toward maintainable code. default, init_project() also initializes renv git new project. means project reproducible version-controlled first commit. renv git default? renv ensures packages project depends recorded reproducible — someone else (future self) can restore exact environment. git provides full history changes, making possible recover mistakes understand project evolved. much easier set start retrofit later.
code: library(toolero)  init_project("~/Documents/my-project") my-project/ ├── data/         # input data ├── data-raw/     # original, unprocessed data ├── R/            # reusable functions ├── scripts/      # analysis scripts ├── plots/        # generated visualizations ├── images/       # static images and assets ├── results/      # processed outputs and tables └── docs/         # notes, manuscripts, Quarto documents

title: Getting started with toolero
text: project needs folders beyond defaults, pass character vector via extra_folders:
code: init_project(   "~/Documents/my-project",   extra_folders = c("notebooks", "presentations") )

what: Opting out of renv or git
title: Getting started with toolero
text: need skip one : might skip renv git? Skipping occasionally useful teaching demonstration contexts overhead full setup unnecessary. project plan share, archive, return later, defaults strongly recommended.
code: init_project(   "~/Documents/my-project",   use_renv = FALSE,   use_git  = FALSE )

title: Getting started with toolero
text: Reading CSV file R straightforward — column names come back spaces, mixed capitalization, special characters. Cleaning small recurring friction point.

title: Getting started with toolero
text: read_clean_csv() combines readr::read_csv() janitor::clean_names() single call: Column names automatically converted lowercase underscores — consistent, predictable, tidyverse-friendly. column called First Name becomes first_name. Q1 Revenue ($) becomes q1_revenue. default, column type messages readr suppressed keep output clean. want see — useful reading unfamiliar dataset first time — set verbose = TRUE: suppress messages default? Column type messages helpful first exploring dataset. script document runs repeatedly, become noise. verbose argument gives control without requiring remember readr option name.
code: data <- read_clean_csv("data/my-file.csv") data <- read_clean_csv("data/my-file.csv", verbose = TRUE)

title: Getting started with toolero
text: toolero intentionally small right now. two functions provides solve specific, recurring problem — getting project started correctly. Future versions may include: Utilities common data validation patterns Helpers writing reproducible reports Functions ease transition local analysis running code remote computing infrastructure goal comprehensive. make right habits easy reach first line code.

title: Getting started with toolero
text: use toolero work, please cite :
code: citation("toolero")

code: @Manual{,   title = {toolero: A Toolkit for Research Workflows in R},   author = {Erwin Lares},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.0},   url = {https://github.com/erwinlares/toolero},   doi = {10.5281/zenodo.19457647}, }

title: A Toolkit for Research Workflows
text: toolero R package designed help researchers implement best practices coding projects. provides small set opinionated, practical functions reduce friction start project day--day data work.

title: A Toolkit for Research Workflows
text: can install toolero GitHub :
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("erwinlares/toolero")

title: A Toolkit for Research Workflows
text: Creates new R project standard folder structure suited research workflows. Optionally initializes renv package management git version control. default folder structure includes: data/, data-raw/, R/, scripts/, plots/, images/, results/, docs/.
code: library(toolero)  # Create a project with the standard folder structure init_project("~/Documents/my-project")  # Add extra folders init_project("~/Documents/my-project", extra_folders = c("notebooks", "presentations"))  # Skip renv and git init_project("~/Documents/my-project", use_renv = FALSE, use_git = FALSE)

title: A Toolkit for Research Workflows
text: Reads CSV file cleans column names one step, producing tidyverse-friendly tibble.
code: library(toolero)  data <- read_clean_csv("path/to/file.csv")  # Show column type messages data <- read_clean_csv("path/to/file.csv", verbose = TRUE)

title: A Toolkit for Research Workflows
text: Identifies three execution environments code currently running : interactive R session, quarto render call, plain Rscript invocation. Returns one "interactive", "quarto", "rscript".
code: library(toolero)  context <- detect_execution_context()  input_file <- switch(context,   interactive = "data/sample.csv",   quarto      = params$input_file,   rscript     = commandArgs(trailingOnly = TRUE)[1] )

title: A Toolkit for Research Workflows
text: Scaffolds new Quarto document reproducible template, including sample dataset UW-Madison branded assets. Optionally pre-populates YAML header user-supplied YAML config file.
code: library(toolero)  # Create with placeholder YAML create_qmd(path = "~/Documents/my-project")  # Create with a custom filename create_qmd(path = "~/Documents/my-project", filename = "report.qmd")  # Pre-populate YAML from a personal config file create_qmd(path = "~/Documents/my-project", yaml_data = "~/my_config.yml")

title: A Toolkit for Research Workflows
text: Splits data frame single grouping column writes group separate CSV file. Filenames derived sanitized group values. Optionally writes manifest.csv listing output files, group values, row counts.
code: library(toolero)  # Load the bundled sample dataset sample_path <- system.file("templates", "sample.csv", package = "toolero") penguins    <- read_clean_csv(sample_path)  # Split by species write_by_group(penguins, group_col = "species", output_dir = tempdir())  # Also write a manifest write_by_group(penguins, group_col = "species",                output_dir = tempdir(), manifest = TRUE)

title: A Toolkit for Research Workflows
text: cite toolero publications:
code: citation("toolero")

title: A Toolkit for Research Workflows

what: Create a new Quarto document from a template — create_qmd
title: Create a new Quarto document from a template — create_qmd
text: Creates new Quarto document specified directory, along sample dataset UW-Madison branded assets. Optionally pre-populates YAML header user-supplied metadata.

title: Create a new Quarto document from a template — create_qmd
code: create_qmd(   path = NULL,   filename = "analysis.qmd",   yaml_data = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE )

title: Create a new Quarto document from a template — create_qmd
text: path string NULL. Path directory document created. NULL, user must supply path explicitly. filename string. Name generated .qmd file. Defaults "analysis.qmd". yaml_data string NULL. Path YAML file containing metadata pre-populate document header. NULL (default), template copied -placeholder prompts intact. overwrite logical. Whether overwrite existing files. Defaults FALSE.

title: Create a new Quarto document from a template — create_qmd
text: Invisibly returns path.

title: Create a new Quarto document from a template — create_qmd
text: create_qmd() performs following steps: Validates path exists. Creates data/ folder path copies sample.csv . Checks assets/styles.css assets/header.html - creates assets/ folder needed copies package. Copies template .qmd path/filename. yaml_data provided, reads YAML file substitutes values document header. Note: path default value. Always supply explicit path avoid writing files unexpected locations. Use tempdir() temporary output testing exploration.

title: Create a new Quarto document from a template — create_qmd
code: # \donttest{ # Create with placeholder YAML in a temp directory create_qmd(path = tempdir()) #> ✔ Created /tmp/RtmpD2mH2m/data/sample.csv #> ✔ Created /tmp/RtmpD2mH2m/assets/styles.css #> ✔ Created /tmp/RtmpD2mH2m/assets/header.html #> ✔ Created /tmp/RtmpD2mH2m/analysis.qmd  # Create with a custom filename create_qmd(path = tempdir(), filename = "report.qmd", overwrite = TRUE) #> ✔ Created /tmp/RtmpD2mH2m/data/sample.csv #> ✔ Created /tmp/RtmpD2mH2m/assets/styles.css #> ✔ Created /tmp/RtmpD2mH2m/assets/header.html #> ✔ Created /tmp/RtmpD2mH2m/report.qmd  # Create with pre-populated YAML yaml_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".yml") writeLines("author:\n  - name: 'Your Name'", yaml_file) create_qmd(path = tempdir(), yaml_data = yaml_file, overwrite = TRUE) #> ✔ Created /tmp/RtmpD2mH2m/data/sample.csv #> ✔ Created /tmp/RtmpD2mH2m/assets/styles.css #> ✔ Created /tmp/RtmpD2mH2m/assets/header.html #> ✔ Created /tmp/RtmpD2mH2m/analysis.qmd # }

what: Detect the current execution context — detect_execution_context
title: Detect the current execution context — detect_execution_context
text: Identifies three execution environments code currently running : interactive R session, quarto render call, plain Rscript invocation. useful writing code behaves correctly across three contexts, resolving input file paths portable way.

title: Detect the current execution context — detect_execution_context
code: detect_execution_context(interactive_fn = interactive)

title: Detect the current execution context — detect_execution_context
text: interactive_fn function. Used detect whether session interactive. Defaults base::interactive. Override tests simulate different execution environments.

title: Detect the current execution context — detect_execution_context
text: character string, one "interactive", "quarto", "rscript".

title: Detect the current execution context — detect_execution_context
text: Detection follows priority order: interactive() TRUE, returns "interactive". environment variable QUARTO_DOCUMENT_PATH set non-empty, returns "quarto". Otherwise, returns "rscript".

title: Detect the current execution context — detect_execution_context
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ context <- detect_execution_context()  input_file <- switch(context,   interactive = "data/sample.csv",   quarto      = params$input_file,   rscript     = commandArgs(trailingOnly = TRUE)[1] ) } # }

what: Initialize a new R project with a standard folder structure — init_project
title: Initialize a new R project with a standard folder structure — init_project
text: init_project() creates new R project given path opinionated folder structure suited research workflows. optionally initializes renv package management git version control.

title: Initialize a new R project with a standard folder structure — init_project
code: init_project(   file_path,   use_renv = TRUE,   use_git = TRUE,   extra_folders = NULL,   open = TRUE,   uw_branding = FALSE )

title: Initialize a new R project with a standard folder structure — init_project
text: file_path character string path name new project (e.g., "~/Documents/-project"). use_renv Logical. TRUE, initializes renv new project. Defaults TRUE. use_git Logical. TRUE, initializes git repository new project. Defaults TRUE. extra_folders character vector additional folder names create inside project. Defaults NULL. open Logical. TRUE, opens new project RStudio creation. Defaults TRUE. uw_branding Logical. TRUE, creates assets/ folder populates UW-Madison RCI branding files (styles.css, header.html, rci-banner.png). Defaults FALSE.

title: Initialize a new R project with a standard folder structure — init_project
text: Called side effects. return value.

title: Initialize a new R project with a standard folder structure — init_project
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Create a project with the standard folder structure init_project("~/Documents/my-project")  # Create a project with UW-Madison RCI branding files init_project("~/Documents/my-project", uw_branding = TRUE)  # Create a project with an additional folder init_project("~/Documents/my-project", extra_folders = c("notebooks"))  # Create a project without renv or git init_project("~/Documents/my-project", use_renv = FALSE, use_git = FALSE) } # }

what: Read and clean a CSV file — read_clean_csv
title: Read and clean a CSV file — read_clean_csv
text: read_clean_csv() reads CSV file cleans column names one step. leverages readr::read_csv() reading janitor::clean_names() making column names tidyverse-friendly (lowercase, spaces, special characters). default, column type messages suppressed. Set verbose = TRUE display .

title: Read and clean a CSV file — read_clean_csv
code: read_clean_csv(file_path, verbose = FALSE)

title: Read and clean a CSV file — read_clean_csv
text: file_path character string path CSV file. verbose Logical. TRUE, displays column type messages readr::read_csv(). Defaults FALSE.

title: Read and clean a CSV file — read_clean_csv
text: tibble clean column names.

title: Read and clean a CSV file — read_clean_csv
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Read and clean a CSV file silently data <- read_clean_csv("path/to/file.csv")  # Read and clean a CSV file with column type messages displayed data <- read_clean_csv("path/to/file.csv", verbose = TRUE) } # }

what: Split a data frame by a grouping column and write each group to a CSV file — write_by_group
title: Split a data frame by a grouping column and write each group to a CSV file — write_by_group
text: Splits data frame single grouping column writes group separate CSV file. Optionally writes manifest file listing output files, group values, row counts.

title: Split a data frame by a grouping column and write each group to a CSV file — write_by_group
code: write_by_group(data, group_col, output_dir = NULL, manifest = FALSE)

title: Split a data frame by a grouping column and write each group to a CSV file — write_by_group
text: data data frame tibble split save. group_col string. name column group . output_dir string NULL. Path directory output files written. Created exist. NULL, user must supply path explicitly. manifest logical. Whether write manifest.csv file output_dir listing output files, group values, row counts. Defaults FALSE.

title: Split a data frame by a grouping column and write each group to a CSV file — write_by_group
text: Invisibly returns output_dir.

title: Split a data frame by a grouping column and write each group to a CSV file — write_by_group
text: Output filenames derived group values group_col. Values sanitized use filenames: converted lowercase, spaces special characters replaced _, consecutive underscores collapsed, leading/trailing underscores stripped. manifest = TRUE, manifest.csv written output_dir containing three columns: group_value, n_rows, file_path. Note: output_dir default value. Always supply explicit path avoid writing files unexpected locations. Use tempdir() temporary output testing exploration.

title: Split a data frame by a grouping column and write each group to a CSV file — write_by_group
code: # \donttest{ # Split a small data frame by group and write to a temp directory data <- data.frame(   species = c("Adelie", "Adelie", "Gentoo"),   mass    = c(3750, 3800, 5000) ) write_by_group(data, group_col = "species", output_dir = tempdir()) #> ✔ Written "Adelie" (2 rows) to /tmp/RtmpD2mH2m/adelie.csv #> ✔ Written "Gentoo" (1 rows) to /tmp/RtmpD2mH2m/gentoo.csv  # Same but also write a manifest write_by_group(data, group_col = "species",                output_dir = tempdir(), manifest = TRUE) #> ✔ Written "Adelie" (2 rows) to /tmp/RtmpD2mH2m/adelie.csv #> ✔ Written "Gentoo" (1 rows) to /tmp/RtmpD2mH2m/gentoo.csv #> ✔ Manifest written to /tmp/RtmpD2mH2m/manifest.csv # }

title: toolero 0.2.0
text: Added detect_execution_context() identify whether code running interactive R session, quarto render call, plain Rscript invocation. Returns one "interactive", "quarto", "rscript". Added create_qmd() scaffold new Quarto document reproducible template, including sample dataset, UW-Madison branded assets, three-context input resolution via detect_execution_context(). Optionally pre-populates YAML header user-supplied YAML config file. Added write_by_group() split data frame single grouping column write group separate CSV file. Filenames derived sanitized group values. Optionally writes manifest.csv listing output files, group values, row counts. Resubmission addressing CRAN feedback: removed redundant “R” title, replaced \dontrun{} \donttest{} throughout, removed default write paths create_qmd() write_by_group(), replaced non-ASCII characters source files.

title: toolero 0.1.1
text: Added uw_branding argument init_project(). TRUE, creates assets/ folder new project populates UW-Madison RCI branding files (styles.css, header.html, rci-banner.png).

title: toolero 0.1.0
text: Initial CRAN submission.
